sections.section: Formerly CODEX STAN C-5-1966. Adopted in 2001. Revised in 2007. Amended in 2008, 2010, 2013, 2018, 2019, 2022.
sections.summary: Provides the document history: formerly CODEX STAN C-5-1966, adopted 2001, revised 2007 and amended in 2008, 2010, 2013, 2018, 2019 and 2022.
Records the 2022 amendment replacing prior non-retail container labelling text with a requirement to follow the General Standard for the Labelling of Non‑Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346-2021).
Scope: applies to Gouda intended for direct consumption or for further processing as described in Section 2.

Describes Gouda as a ripened firm/semi‑hard cheese with specified colour, firm texture and typically round gas holes up to ~10 mm, sold in defined shapes and rind conditions, and states normal ripening (about 3 weeks at 10–17°C) though alternative ripening yielding equivalent properties is permitted; Gouda for further processing and cheeses <2.5 kg need not exhibit the same degree of ripening.

Sets compositional limits: minimum milk fat in dry matter 30% with reference FDM ranges and corresponding minimum dry matter levels (e.g. 48% DM for 30–40% FDM, up to 62% DM for ≥60% FDM); allows Gouda with 40–48% FDM and weight <2.5 kg to be sold as 'baby' with minimum DM 50%; compositional changes beyond these minima/maxima are non‑compliant with the General Standard for the Use of Dairy Terms.

Permits only additive functional classes justified in the standard and in accordance with the General Standard for Food Additives (CXS 192-1995), specifying which classes are justified for cheese mass and surface/rind (e.g. colours and acidity regulators for cheese mass, preservatives for mass and surface, anti‑caking for surface/rind).

Requires products and the milk used in their manufacture to comply with Maximum Levels for contaminants and toxins and with CAC maximum residue limits for veterinary drugs and pesticides as set out in the General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193‑1995).

Recommends that production and handling follow the General Principles of Food Hygiene (CXC 1-1969), the Code of Hygienic Practice for Milk and Milk Products (CXC 57-2004) and other relevant Codex texts, and that products meet any established microbiological criteria per CXG 21‑1997.

Specifies labelling provisions in addition to general Codex standards, covering permitted use of the name 'Gouda' only for cheeses conforming to the Standard, country of origin declaration, milk fat declaration options, a date‑marking exception for non‑final consumer products, and non‑retail container labelling requirements.

States that the name 'Gouda' may be used only if the product conforms to this Standard, requires appropriate qualification when fat content is outside the reference range but above the absolute minimum, and allows the designation for cut, sliced, shredded or grated products made from compliant cheese.

Requires declaration of country of origin (the country of manufacture), and where substantial transformation occurs in a second country that country is to be shown as the origin for labelling purposes.

Mandates declaration of milk fat content in a manner acceptable in the country of retail sale, either as percentage by mass, percentage of fat in dry matter, or grams per serving (with number of servings stated).
Allows the date of manufacture to be declared instead of minimum durability information for products not intended to be purchased by the final consumer.
States that labelling of non‑retail containers should follow the General Standard for the Labelling of Non‑Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346‑2021).

Normative provision: compliance with this Standard must be checked using the methods of analysis and sampling set out in the Codex Recommended Methods of Analysis and Sampling (CXS 234-1999) relevant to the Standard's provisions.
Informative appendix (non‑normative) for Gouda cheese giving additional product information: typical wheel weights 2.5–30 kg (smaller labeled 'Baby') and a note that the salting method is brine.
